---
title: Filters
---

Filters handle fenced divs and bracketed spans that need logic beyond `{{variable}}` substitution: conditional rendering, computed values, content rewriting.


# External filters

An external filter is an executable at `_calepin/filters/{class}` (or `_calepin/filters/{class}.{ext}` for format-specific variants). It receives flat JSON on stdin and writes the rendered output to stdout.

For a div:

```json
{
  "context": "div",
  "content": "<p>rendered children</p>",
  "classes": ["myclass"],
  "id": "my-id",
  "format": "html",
  "title": "user attribute"
}
```

For a span:

```json
{
  "context": "span",
  "content": "span text",
  "classes": ["myclass"],
  "id": "",
  "format": "html",
  "tip": "user attribute"
}
```

Exit 0: stdout is used as final output (template is skipped). Exit non-zero: falls through to next handler.

## Example: a div filter

`_calepin/filters/accordion` (Python):

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
title = data.get("title", "Details")
content = data["content"]
fmt = data["format"]

if fmt == "html":
    print(f'<details><summary>{title}</summary>{content}</details>')
elif fmt == "tex":
    print(f'\\paragraph{{{title}}} {content}')
else:
    print(f'**{title}**\n\n{content}')
```

Usage:

    ::: {.accordion title="Click to expand"}
    Hidden content here.
    :::

::: {.accordion title="Click to expand"}
Hidden content here.
:::

## Example: a span filter

`_calepin/filters/tooltip` (Python):

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
text = data["content"]
tip = data.get("tip", "")
fmt = data["format"]

if fmt == "html":
    print(f'<span class="tooltip" title="{tip}">{text}</span>')
else:
    print(text)
```

Usage: `This has [hover me]{.tooltip tip="Extra info"} inline.`

## Example: wordcount filter

`_calepin/filters/wordcount` (Python):

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys, re

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
content = data["content"]
plain = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", content)
count = len(plain.split())

badge = f'<p style="font-size: 0.85em; color: #666;">({count} words)</p>'
print(f'<div class="wordcount">{badge}\n{content}</div>')
```

::: {.wordcount}
This paragraph is processed by the wordcount filter. The filter strips HTML tags from the rendered children, counts the words, and prepends a small badge showing the total. Filters have full programmatic control over the output, unlike templates which only do variable substitution.
:::


# Built-in filters

## TheoremFilter

Handles theorem-type classes and maintains per-type counters. See [Math](math.qmd#theorem-environments) for the full list of supported types, cross-reference prefixes, and template customization.

## CalloutFilter

Enriches `callout-note`, `callout-tip`, `callout-warning`, `callout-important`, and `callout-caution` divs with `{{title}}`, `{{icon}}`, `{{collapse}}`, and `{{appearance}}` variables.


# Processing order

When *Calepin* encounters a fenced div or bracketed span:

1. **WASM plugins** (in front matter order)
2. **External filters** (`_calepin/filters/`)
3. **Built-in filters** (TheoremFilter, CalloutFilter)
4. **Template** (by class name, then `div.{ext}`)
5. **Default fallback** (generic markup)

A handler that produces output short-circuits the pipeline.


# Built-in div classes

## `.hidden`

Processes content (R code executes, variables are set) but produces no output:

::: {.hidden}
```{r}
hidden_value <- 99
```
:::

The variable is available: `{r} hidden_value`.


# Cross-references

See [Math](math.qmd#cross-references) for theorem cross-reference prefixes and label resolution.
